prompt: A highly detailed layered papercut diorama illustration of a deer family standing on a narrow forest path in deep winter. Show exactly 4 deer facing forward: 1 large stag with tall branching antlers positioned slightly right of center, 1 adult doe on the left, 1 smaller young deer in the middle foreground, and 1 young deer on the right. The scene is built entirely in intricate cut-paper craft style with stacked paper layers, crisp cut edges, sculpted contours, and soft dimensional shadows between layers. The animals are made from warm brown, chestnut, tan, and cream paper with delicately layered chest fur, white muzzles, alert ears, and small patches of snow resting on their heads, backs, and shoulders. Surround them with a dense winter forest of tall bare trees and fir trees, all rendered in layered paper silhouettes. Snow heavily coats the branches, bushes, rocks, and ground. Compose the trees as an enclosing corridor that frames the deer symmetrically, with the snowy path leading from the foreground into the group. Use a restrained cold palette for the environment: white, off-white, pale gray, slate gray, and charcoal, contrasted by the warm brown deer. Add subtle depth haze in the distant trees, clean studio-like lighting, and a calm storybook atmosphere. Ultra-detailed craftsmanship, elegant winter scene, realistic paper texture, refined 3D paper sculpture look, vertical composition.
